req.title: External ACH transfer (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.
preconditions: ach_link verified

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-029 defines a hard limit of $10,000 per ACH transfer with step-up verification required above that threshold. BVA drives tests at $0.01 (min valid), $10,000.00 (exact upper boundary - allowed), and $10,000.01 (just above - rejected without step-up). EP partitions the valid transfer range ($0.01–$10,000) vs. the invalid range (>$10,000 without step-up) and the insufficient-funds partition. DT covers the interaction between amount, step-up flag, and account balance. NEG covers the 422 and 409 error pathways. STC verifies the transition from PENDING to QUEUED on a successful transfer. Together these seven tests achieve full boundary, equivalence, decision, state, and negative coverage.

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_valid_mid_range
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid session token
ACH link is verified for destination account
Source account balance >= $500.00
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=500.00, a valid verified ach_account_id, and a valid auth token
expected: Confirmation event is emitted (checked via response confirmation_ref field)
trace: R-FIN-029: valid amount within $0.01–$10,000 partition shall be accepted and queued
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_valid_mid_range(api_client, verified_ach_account, funded_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": funded_account["id"],
        "destination_ach_account_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": "500.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "QUEUED"
    assert "confirmation_ref" in body

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_exact_upper_boundary_allowed
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid session token
ACH link is verified for destination account
Source account balance >= $10,000.00
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000.00 (exact upper limit) and no step-up token
trace: R-FIN-029: transfer at exactly $10,000.00 shall be accepted without step-up verification
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_exact_upper_boundary_allowed(api_client, verified_ach_account, funded_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": funded_account["id"],
        "destination_ach_account_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": "10000.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "QUEUED"
    assert body.get("step_up_required") is not True

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_rejected_without_stepup
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid session token
ACH link is verified for destination account
Source account balance >= $10,000.01
No step-up verification token present in request
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000.01 and no step_up_token header
trace: R-FIN-029: amount > $10,000 without step-up shall return 422
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_rejected_without_stepup(api_client, verified_ach_account, funded_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": funded_account["id"],
        "destination_ach_account_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": "10000.01",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "STEP_UP_REQUIRED"
    assert "transfer_id" not in body

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_above_limit_accepted_with_stepup
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid session token
ACH link is verified for destination account
Source account balance >= $15,000.00
A valid step-up verification token has been obtained
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=15000.00 and a valid step_up_token in the request header
trace: R-FIN-029: amount > $10,000 WITH valid step-up shall be accepted and queued

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid session token
ACH link is verified for destination account
Source account balance is $50.00 (insufficient for transfer)
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=500.00 where source balance < requested amount
trace: R-FIN-029: 409 error pathway when source account has insufficient funds
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409(api_client, verified_ach_account, underfunded_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": underfunded_account["id"],
        "destination_ach_account_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": "500.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"
    assert "transfer_id" not in body

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_state_transition_pending_to_queued
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid session token
ACH link is verified for destination account
Source account balance >= $1,000.00
expected: POST returns HTTP 202 with status = QUEUED
Subsequent GET returns the same transfer_id with status = QUEUED or PROCESSING
Status is never FAILED or REJECTED on this path
trace: R-FIN-029: ACH batch queued expected outcome; PENDING -> QUEUED state transition
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_state_transition_pending_to_queued(api_client, verified_ach_account, funded_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": funded_account["id"],
        "destination_ach_account_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": "1000.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    post_resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 202
    transfer_id = post_resp.json()["transfer_id"]
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/transfers/ach/{transfer_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    status = get_resp.json()["status"]
    assert status in ("QUEUED", "PROCESSING")
    assert status not in ("FAILED", "REJECTED")

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_unverified_ach_link_rejected
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid session token
ACH link exists but is NOT verified (pending micro-deposit confirmation)
Source account balance >= $500.00
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=500.00 using an unverified ach_account_id
trace: R-FIN-029: precondition ach_link verified must hold; unverified link violates precondition
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_unverified_ach_link_rejected(api_client, unverified_ach_account, funded_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": funded_account["id"],
        "destination_ach_account_id": unverified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": "500.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "ACH_LINK_NOT_VERIFIED"
    assert "transfer_id" not in body
